title: Soy wax jar candle with vanilla
subtitle: A 200 ml container candle with a warm vanilla fragrance oil
excerpt: A soy wax container candle in a 200 ml glass jar with a pre-tabbed cotton wick and 6% vanilla fragrance oil by wax weight. Burns approximately 30-35 hours. The first burn sets the candle's memory; take it to a full melt pool before extinguishing.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry candle-making guides (brambleberry.com, accessed May 2026): soy wax fragrance load and pour temperature. CandleScience container soy notes (candlescience.com, accessed May 2026): wick sizing by jar diameter.

content.text: A soy wax container candle scented with vanilla fragrance oil. Soy wax is a vegetable wax with a melt point around 50°C, lower than paraffin, and a clean burn. It holds fragrance well at 6-8% of wax weight, which is the range for consistent hot throw without the fragrance oil destabilising the wax. Vanilla fragrance oil gives a warm, rounded scent that works year-round.

Melt wax over a simmering-water bath, not directly on the hob. Wax above its flash point can ignite; water will make a wax fire worse. Keep a kitchen fire blanket within reach and never leave melting wax unattended.

items.name: Soy wax (container blend)
items.prepNote: Container-grade soy wax. A 200 ml jar takes approximately 140-150 g at a standard fill.

items.name: Candle fragrance oil
items.prepNote: Vanilla candle-grade fragrance oil. 6% of wax weight (9 g). IFRA-rated for container candles. Add when wax has cooled to 55°C.

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: Pre-tabbed cotton wick, CD-12 or equivalent for a 7 cm diameter jar. Stick the metal tab to the base of the clean, dry jar with a small spot of hot glue or a wick sticker.

content.text: Stick the wick tab to the centre of the clean, dry jar base. Thread the wick through the centring bar and rest it across the jar top, pulling the wick straight and centred. A wick that sits off-centre will burn unevenly.

Weigh the soy wax into the candle pouring pitcher. Set it inside a saucepan of simmering water. Stir occasionally as the wax melts. The melt point is around 50°C; the pitcher will be noticeably warm but not scalding when the wax is fully liquid. Allow the wax to cool in the pitcher to 55-65°C before adding the fragrance oil.
Weigh the fragrance oil directly into the wax at the correct

(55-65°C). Stir gently for 2 minutes, reaching the sides and base of the pitcher. A slow stir embeds the fragrance rather than aerating the wax. Pour slowly into the prepared jar, keeping the wick central. Fill to about 1 cm below the jar rim.

Allow the candle to cool undisturbed at room temperature for at least 24 hours. If a small sinkhole forms around the wick as the wax contracts, reheat a small amount of wax and top up. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.
. Allow the melt pool to reach the jar edge before extinguishing, which takes 3-4 hours for a 7 cm jar. Candles stopped before the melt pool fills the jar will tunnel on every subsequent burn.

Store unlit candles in a cool, dark place away from direct sunlight, which fades the wax colour and the fragrance. Soy wax does not go off; an unlit candle is effectively indefinite. The vanilla fragrance fades after about 2 years. Burn within 2 years for the best scent. Trim the wick to 5 mm before each burn; a long wick mushrooms, smokes, and increases soot.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 200 ml jar candle (~30-35 burn hours)
